Ad Text:
Through the merger of Bank Australia and Qudos Bank we’re creating a better, stronger and more resilient bank for our customers and employees. As one company with two trusted brands, we’re building on the strong foundations of our history and shared values as we bring together the best of both banks.

The Product Advisor – Integration is responsible for managing, coordinating and executing Bank Australia’s product integration activities, with a key focus on delivering the Significant Financial Institution (SFI) uplift program across retail banking products and related processes. The role acts as a bridge between product strategy and execution, ensuring successful delivery of product changes, new developments and project workstreams while maintaining strong adherence to product governance frameworks. The Product Advisor also supports the delivery of broader strategic product integration initiatives as required.

Why join us

We have big plans to become Australia’s most trusted bank and a leading purpose-driven business.
As a certified B Corp, we’re part of a global business community who meet the highest social and environmental standards, putting purpose over profit.
Every role and person in our bank is essential to bringing our values, purpose and aspiration to life.
We offer flexible working options, competitive salary and 14% super.
Our staff have access to a range of meaningful benefits to support their physical and mental wellbeing including 24/7 free counselling, free flu vaccinations and discounted gym memberships.
We also support staff with study assistance, paid parental leave (regardless of gender), volunteer, bereavement, gender affirmation and family violence leave; and the opportunity for bonus annual leave.
We support our staff to grow their careers through learning and development and an organisational culture where we reward and recognise innovation and values, not long hours.
We are committed to building an inclusive culture and a diverse workforce that reflects the Australian community.
We care for a conservation reserve – where we work with key partner organisations to protect threatened species and involve staff in conservation.

What you will be doing

Coordinate and lead product requirements for the enterprise‑wide SFI uplift program, ensuring alignment across retail product portfolios.
Manage end‑to‑end delivery of product changes and integration activities, including defining requirements, planning timelines, managing scope and ensuring high‑quality execution.
Liaise with cross‑functional stakeholders to provide updates, KPIs, progress reporting and visibility of product delivery milestones.
Translate prudential, risk and operational SFI requirements into clear product, process and control changes, ensuring regulatory alignment and effective change management.
Support program governance by contributing to evidence gathering, traceability, control uplift and adherence to the Product Governance Framework.
Deliver SFI‑related and integration initiatives on time and to required regulatory and quality standards.
Maintain accurate and complete product documentation, controls and process artefacts.
Build and nurture strong relationships with internal teams (Product, Strategy, PMO, Risk & Compliance, Legal, Customer, Technology, Finance, Lending) and external partners such as consultants and auditors.

What you will bring

Strong ownership of deliverables with the ability to manage dependencies, prioritise effectively and meet deadlines.
Excellent relationship‑building, communication and influencing skills across cross‑functional teams.
Proactive, resilient and agile mindset with a strong alignment to the bank’s values and continuous‑improvement focus.
Ability to interpret prudential and regulatory requirements and translate them into practical product, process and control changes.
Strong analytical, risk‑assessment and problem‑solving capability, providing clear product and delivery advice.
Solid understanding of banking products, product operations, product risk and change delivery within regulated environments.
Familiarity with product systems and data flows across deposits, payments and digital channels.
Strong experience in product management, business analysis, project delivery or related roles within financial services or regulated industries.
Relevant tertiary qualifications in product, business, commerce, project or risk management (desirable).
Proficiency in Microsoft Office and project management tools such as Jira.
Commitment to Bank Australia’s purpose, aspiration, values and brand as well as the B Corp ethos.

By submitting an application you agree to Bank Australia’s Privacy Policy for applicants and confirm that you are legally able to work in Australia. Bank Australia is an equal opportunity employer committed to sustainable development. We encourage people from different backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, people from different cultural backgrounds and people with disabilities because we want to reflect the diversity of our communities. We are committed to making reasonable adjustments to provide a positive, barrier-free recruitment process and supportive workplace.

Bank Australia reserves the right to withdraw this advertisement at any time. Candidates will be progressed as applications are received, rather than after the advertisement closure date. If you wish to be considered, please submit your application as soon as possible. Please note, the successful candidate will be subject to satisfactory background checks including but not limited to police and previous employment.

Talenza have been engaged by an Australian clean energy company to find an Information Architect to work across a data uplift program of work. The successful candidate will be an experienced data/information architect who has worked with modern microsoft data technologies.

This role requires 3 days per week in the Brisbane office and is only open to candidates currently based in Brisbane with full working rights.



Benefits

Contract opportunity
Competitive day rate up to $1175 + GST
Hybrid working environment (3 days per week in office)
Amazing leadership group and office/team culture
Global ASX listed enterprise
Clean Energy Organisation





Key Requirements

Strong experience designing and governing enterprise data and information architectures that support secure, trusted and accessible data across operational and analytical platforms.
Expertise in cloud data platforms, preferably Microsoft Azure, including services such as Azure Synapse, Data Lake, Data Factory, Azure SQL and Event Hub.
Proven capability in data architecture and engineering, including data modelling, data warehouse/lakehouse design, ETL/ELT pipelines, streaming data and data migration.
Experience designing enterprise integration architectures, including APIs, messaging/service bus patterns and integration between operational systems and analytics platforms.
Strong knowledge of enterprise analytics and reporting platforms, particularly Power BI, including platform architecture, performance monitoring and user adoption.
Experience developing and implementing information architecture and governance, including information classification, taxonomy, ontology and enterprise information asset structuring.
Demonstrated expertise in data governance, data quality, privacy and compliance, including data cataloguing and lineage tools such as Microsoft Purview.
Familiarity with advanced analytics platforms and technologies, including AI/ML capabilities, LLM use cases and programming languages such as Python, R and SQL.
Experience working with DevOps and modern delivery practices, including code control, CI/CD pipelines, platform monitoring and operational performance management.




Thank you for your interest, at Talenza we are passionate about providing equal employment opportunities and embracing diversity. We encourage people with disabilities and those from diverse backgrounds to apply. However, please note that we are only able to proceed with those who have full rights to work in Australia. Please apply directly or for a quicker response, contact Lewis Babcock (Talenza) through LinkedIn.

Job Title: Program Risk Analyst and Reporting Coordinator
Ad Text:
Extraordinary teams building inspiring projects:

Since 1898, we have helped customers complete more than 25,000 projects in 160 countries on all seven continents that have created jobs, grown economies, improved the resiliency of the world's infrastructure, increased access to energy, resources, and vital services, and made the world a safer, cleaner place. 

Differentiated by the quality of our people and our relentless drive to deliver the most successful outcomes, we align our capabilities to our customers' objectives to create a lasting positive impact. We serve the Infrastructure; Nuclear, Security & Environmental; Energy; Mining & Metals, and the Manufacturing and Technology markets. Our services span from initial planning and investment, through start-up and operations. 

Core to Bechtel is our Vision, Values and Commitments. They are what we believe, what customers can expect, and how we deliver.

Project Overview:

Bechtel helps clients deliver projects of purpose that create a lasting positive legacy globally. These large, complex and multi-disciplined projects create jobs and grow economies; improve the resiliency of the world’s infrastructure; connect communities to resources and opportunities; get us closer to net zero; tackle critical environmental challenges to protect people and the planet; and accelerate progress to make the world a cleaner, greener, safer place.  Bechtel is providing a wide variety project and contract management services, crossing all disciplines, to customers in Western Australia to support exciting capital works programmes, including in aviation, defence and energy infrastructure. We are looking for people to join us in a long-term relationship to deliver these services in and around Perth where we are working within integrated teams with our customers and to help build Bechtel’s capabilities for even greater opportunities in the future

Job Summary:

As the Program Risk Analyst and Reporting Coordinator you will be responsible for supporting Project Risk Management (PRM) and relevant internal and external stakeholders in PRM matters across the assigned program or project. The role requires a people person with ability to drive focus on PRM, influence without authority and tie PRM with key interfaces across the assigned scope.

Major Responsibilities:

Supports implementation of the project risk management program across the assigned program or project. 

Responsible for the development, deployment, and management of PRM data collection, dashboards, analysis tools and reports.

Maintain risk and control registers and assist with risk reporting across the program.

Responsible for quantitative risk analysis (schedule and cost) for assigned program.

Supports the enterprise analysis of project risks.

Support and maintain risk activities to ensure alignment with the risk management plan.

Supports, coordinates and runs risk workshops on projects.

Coordinating and consolidating input for regular risk reports and verifies consistency with previous reports to ensure compatibility and consistency

Supports preparation of board papers as required

Coordinates risk reporting practices for compliance with applicable standards

Education and Experience Requirements:

Typically requires bachelor's degree (or international equivalent) and 5-8 years of relevant experience or 9-12 years of relevant work experience.

Skill in working and communicating effectively, both orally and in writing, with other project functional groups. Proven ability to build strong relationships with customers and team members.

Demonstrated experience in qualitative and quantitative risk management is necessary. 

Experience in the infrastructure / heavy civil / aviation / renewables / construction industries advantageous  

High attention to detail and accuracy in managing documents and data.

Total Rewards/Benefits:

For decades, Bechtel has worked to inspire the next generation of employees and beyond! Because our teams face some of the world's toughest challenges, we offer robust benefits to ensure our people thrive.  Whether it is advancing careers, delivering programs to enhance our culture, or providing time to recharge, Bechtel has the benefits to build a legacy of sustainable growth. Learn more at Bechtel Total Rewards

Diverse teams build the extraordinary:

As a global company, Bechtel has long been home to a vibrant multitude of nationalities, cultures, ethnicities, and life experiences. This diversity has made us a more trusted partner, more effective problem solvers and innovators, and a more attractive destination for leading talent.

We are committed to being a company where every colleague feels that they belong-where colleagues feel part of "One Team," respected and rewarded for what they bring, supported in pursuing their goals, invested in our values and purpose, and treated equitably.

Job Title: Principal Consultant - Data and Analytics | Brisbane
Ad Text:
Our Purpose

Here at Datacom, we connect people and technology to solve challenges, create opportunities and discover new possibilities for the communities we live in whilst embodying a culture of trust and respect for employees and customers alike.

Our Team

The Data and Analytics team are a progressive group of diverse and creative individuals with a deep love for all things data related. They build platforms and solutions that are leading edge and at the forefront of the technology curve. A close knit and fun team embedded within our rapidly growing Professional Services division, and part of the wider Datacom ecosystem.

About the Role

As a Principal Consultant, you will lead the technical delivery of large-scale data platform and BI solutions. You will be asked to design, implement and own the delivery of robust Data Platform solutions and end-to-end components. This is a client facing role, so you'll need to be an excellent communicator and good with people. The Principal Consultant works closely with practice leads, project managers, scrum masters, and client stakeholders and teams.

What You’ll Do

Working with clients (businesses users) to gather, clarify requirements and business rules by running workshops with stakeholders.
Lead the technical architecture, design and delivery for a new Data platform.
Provide overall thought leadership to our clients regarding data profiling and integration recommendations.
Data modelling (relational and dimensional like star schema and Snowflake schema)
Managing development life cycle using various source code management tools.
Ability to perform performance tuning and optimisation on new and/or existing data warehouse solutions.
Provide detailed documentation, end user training and knowledge-transfer services to customers, and internal teams.
Mentor developers, acting as an SME in data warehouse architecture and data modelling

What You’ll Bring:

Advanced data modelling skills (Star schema, Snowflake schema, data vault)
Proven track-record of building EDW’s
Advanced ETL /ELT processes design and implementation.
Advanced skills in performance tuning, query plan, query plan analysis, indexing, table partitioning.
Strong skills in cloud platforms such as Azure, AWS & GCP
Strong skills in Azure Data Factory and data Flows  and Azure SQL data platform.
Extensive knowledge of Databricks architecture and features.
Proven track-record  in the design, development and implementation of data solutions using Databricks.
Extensive knowledge of Snowflake’s architecture and features.
Proven track-record  in the design, development and implementation of data solutions using Snowflake.
Experience in data ingestion tools such as Apache Kafka, Informatica, AWS Glue, Azure event hubs, Fivetran, Matillion, DBT and Coalesce.
Experience in Apache Airflow.
Strong consultation and clients’ management skills
Strong understanding of Agile delivery methodology.
Excellent verbal and written communications.

 

Required Skills and Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related discipline
Minimum 8+ years’ experience in EDW design and implementation.
Minimum 8+ years’ experience in ETL architecting and design.
Minimum 8+ years’ experience in data modelling (Star schema & Snowflake schema)
Minimum 5+ years’ experience in semantic layer development.
Minimum 5+ years’ experience in reporting and visualization tools.
Advanced skills in Microsoft BI stack.
Minimum 8+ years’ experience in various Microsoft SQL server technologies including SSIS, SSRS, SSAS
Minimum 4+ years’ experience in design, development and implementation of data solutions using Snowflake.
Minimum 4+ years’ experience in design, development and implementation of data solutions using Databricks.
Wherescape RED, 3D experience is preferred.
Minimum 3+ years’ experience in Power BI.
Advanced skills in Azure, Fabric, and Snowflake pipelines, with experience in tools such as Azure Data Factory, Coalesce, Matillion, dbt, and Fivetran.
Strong skills in data platform implementation, configuration, performance and cost management tuning (i.e. Azure, Fabric, Snowflake).

 

Why join us here at Datacom?

Datacom is one of Australia and New Zealand’s largest suppliers of Information Technology professional services. We have managed to maintain a dynamic, agile, small business feel that is often diluted in larger organisations of our size. It's our people that give Datacom its unique culture and energy that you can feel from the moment you meet with us.

We care about our people and provide a range of perks such as social events, chill-out spaces, remote working, flexi-hours and professional development courses to name a few. You’ll have the opportunity to learn, develop your career, connect and bring your true self to work. You will be recognised and valued for your contributions and be able to do your work in a collegial, flat-structured environment.

We operate at the forefront of technology to help Australia and New Zealand’s largest enterprise organisations explore possibilities and solve their greatest challenges, so you will never run out of interesting new challenges and opportunities.

We want Datacom to be an inclusive and welcoming workplace for everyone and take pride in the steps we have taken and continue to take to make our environment fun and friendly, and our people feel supported.

DXC Technology (NYSE: DXC) is a leading enterprise technology and innovation partner delivering software, services, and solutions to global enterprises and public sector organizations — helping them harness AI to drive outcomes at a time of exponential change with speed. With deep expertise in Managed Infrastructure Services, Application Modernization, and Industry-Specific Software Solutions, DXC modernizes, secures, and operates some of the world’s most complex technology estates. Learn more on dxc.com

We are currently looking for an experienced SAP Integration Senior Consultant – CPI to join our high performing Cloud and Platform team.  This is a permanent position and can be based in Melbourne, Sydney or Canberra. 

In this role you will be involved in a variety of integration activities mainly focused across CPI and PI/PO supporting a range of clients.  Your outstanding communication skills will allow you to develop strong customer relationships and operate at a high-level within our Managed Services team.

To be successful you will require the following;

5 years exp, mainly SAP CPI, but some PI/PO experience is highly regarded.
Strong understanding of integration patterns, concepts and standards across enterprise applications
SAP AIF experience advantageous
Experience in ABAP would be highly beneficial
Strong stakeholder engagement skills
Excellent interpersonal skills
Previous experience working in a consulting environment, working across either projects or BAU 
In alignment with our commitment to fostering a flexible and dynamic work environment, we have adopted a hybrid working approach that acknowledges the importance of in-person collaboration while recognising that the determination of your work location will be based on a range of factors to ensure both individual preferences and operational requirements are met.

If you would like to be part of a culture that drives innovation, delivers results, rewards performance and encourages ideas, then please press the "Apply Now" button to submit your resume.

Who We Are 
At Torrens University Australia, making a difference is in our DNA. As a Certified B Corporation, we belong to a global community of future-focused companies using business as a force for good. We believe in the transformative power of education to change lives, families, communities – and the world. 

As Australia’s fastest-growing university, we are building modern, scalable digital capabilities that support student success, operational excellence and responsible AI adoption. Our Data Platform is central to this ambition — and we’re now looking for a Principal Data Engineer to help shape its future. 

What’s the Job? 
This is a senior technical leadership role with deep hands-on engineering accountability. 

Reporting to the Head of Data Platform & Governance, you will be the technical authority responsible for designing, building and uplifting Torrens’ cloud-native data platform on Google Cloud Platform (GCP). 

The role will begin with a technical assessment of the current data platform to identify resilience gaps, engineering standards, pipeline reliability improvements and opportunities to introduce scalable platform patterns. 

You’ll step into an environment where foundational standards, DataOps maturity, observability and governance uplift are key priorities. This role will: 

Conduct a technical audit of current pipelines, standards and platform resilience 

Define and implement data engineering design standards, reusable patterns and reference architectures 

Build and optimise modern batch and streaming pipelines 

Establish platform-level CI/CD, IaC and observability frameworks 

Lead technical design reviews for internal and vendor-built pipelines 

Ensure solutions align with enterprise architecture and platform standards 

Define platform SLAs and support model with Operations teams 

Implement monitoring, alerting and incident response frameworks 

Uplift engineering capability while remaining hands-on 

This is not a pure architecture role. We are looking for an engineer-first leader — someone who can design at scale, review code, build pipelines and drive technical quality. 

Key Points About This Role 

Cloud-Native Platform Ownership 
Lead architecture and engineering of scalable GCP data pipelines across batch and streaming environments. 

DataOps & Automation Uplift 
Implement CI/CD, IaC (Terraform), testing and observability to reduce incidents and increase resilience. 

Governance-by-Design Engineering 
Embed metadata, lineage, data quality and privacy controls directly into ingestion and pipeline frameworks. 

Technical Authority & Vendor Oversight 
Provide engineering standards, review vendor outputs and guide cross-functional delivery quality. 

Hands-On Leadership 
Mentor engineers, lead technical reviews and build high-quality, reusable data assets. 

Who You Are 

GCP Engineering Expert 
8+ years in data/cloud engineering with strong hands-on experience across BigQuery, Dataflow, Pub/Sub, Composer/Airflow and Cloud Storage. 

Advanced Programming Capability 
Expert in Python and SQL with experience building scalable, distributed data pipelines. 

Data Platform Observability 
Experience implementing monitoring and observability frameworks for data pipelines, including logging, metrics, alerting and pipeline health tracking. 

DataOps & Automation Leader 
Proven delivery of CI/CD pipelines, Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform), observability and automated testing frameworks. 

Data Modelling 
Experience designing analytical data models and optimising schema design for scalable analytical workloads. 

Architecture-Aligned Engineer 
Strong understanding of cloud-native data platform design, streaming architectures and cost optimisation (FinOps). 

Technical Influencer & Mentor 
Confident communicator who can lead code reviews, challenge vendors, uplift standards and operate independently. 

This is a Full-Time, Permanent position based at our Surry Hills Campus in Sydney offering Hybrid working.  

To review the full job description please Click here.  

Why Join Us? 
Bold, modern and agile, we’ve grown from 164 students in 2014 to 40,000+ alumni across 115+ countries. We are building enterprise data capabilities from the ground up — giving you the opportunity to shape standards, define patterns and create a true centre of excellence. 

This role offers architectural influence, hands-on engineering depth and the autonomy to build something meaningful. 

What We Offer 

At Torrens University Australia, you’ll benefit from: 

Study free in a course/degree related to your position Flexible hybrid working arrangements 

B Corp certified university – values driven and purpose led 

Access to internal opportunities to grow and move across the organisation 

A culture that encourages innovation, accountability and impact 

Hiring Process 
We aim to provide you with the information you need at each stage of this process to help you present your best self. If you have any accessibility requirements, please contact your recruiter at Nikeey.silwal@torrens.edu.au for confidential support. 

We are committed to ensuring an equitable, barrier-free application process and have a Reasonable Adjustment Procedure in place to support you throughout the recruitment process. 

We are a 2026 Circle Back Initiative Employer and commit to respond to every applicant. 

To learn more about what makes Torrens University Australia a great place to work, visit https://www.torrens.edu.au/blog. 

We’d love to hear from you.

Technical Business Analyst - Data & Analytics

Australian Citizenship Required
Canberra | Federal Government Environment

We are seeking a Technical Business Analyst to support the delivery of high-quality data and analytics solutions within a fast-paced Federal Government environment.

In this role, you will work closely with policy, operational and technical teams to document complex business processes, define data and business requirements, and support the design of future-state cloud and analytics platforms that enable evidence-based decision making.

The Role

Document complex business processes using BPMN, flowcharts and structured documentation

Conduct gap analysis and define business and data requirements for analytics and cloud-based platforms

Analyse current and future state processes to support data-driven and cloud-enabled solutions

Collaborate with policy, operational and technical stakeholders to co-design platform solutions

Support development of business cases including cost-benefit analysis and risk assessments

Translate complex technical and data concepts into clear business requirements

About You

Proven experience as a Business Analyst in technical, data or analytics environments

Strong capability documenting business processes and requirements

Experience working with cloud and analytics platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud or similar)

Experience with data visualisation and analytics tools such as Power BI, Tableau, SAS or similar

Strong stakeholder engagement and facilitation skills

Ability to work in fast-paced, high-performance environments and manage competing priorities

Australian Citizenship is mandatory

Desirable Experience

Experience supporting government reporting or analytics programs

Understanding of public sector data environments or reporting frameworks

Experience contributing to business cases and organisational transformation initiatives

This role is ideal for a detail-oriented Technical Business Analyst who enjoys working with data, cloud platforms and stakeholders to deliver meaningful insights and modern analytics capabilities.

Apply now to be considered.

Blue Prism Developer - Finance Automation

An exciting opportunity is available for a Blue Prism Developer to join a Financial Systems and Projects team, supporting automation initiatives across Finance and Accounting processes. This is a newly created role focused on implementing automation solutions using Blue Prism, the organisation's approved RPA platform.

This is a hands-on development role suited to someone comfortable working with RPA tools and scripting who can quickly contribute to existing automation projects. With process designs already defined, the primary focus will be on building, implementing, and supporting automation solutions that streamline key financial workflows.

You will collaborate closely with technology teams and global colleagues to automate important financial processes such as bank reconciliations and other operational workflows.

Key Responsibilities

Develop and implement automation solutions using Blue Prism
Support automation of Finance and Accounting processes, including bank reconciliation
Use scripting languages such as Python to enhance automation and data handling
Work with system processes, data, and reporting to support automated workflows
Collaborate with technology teams responsible for databases and infrastructure

Job Title: Senior Advisor - Mine Economics & Financial Modelling
Ad Text:
A transition opportunity for a Private Equity & Investment Banking professional

Looking to apply your investment skillset to real assets and long-term capital decisions?



Mining is one of the few industries where geology, engineering and economics intersect to determine asset value, making it one of the most intellectually interesting sectors for investment professionals.



You will work alongside mining engineers, geologists, decarbonisation and gas management specialists and operational experts to understand how geology, mine planning, processing, cost structures and carbon reduction initiatives drive asset value across critical minerals, bulk commodities and precious metals.



Palaris is seeking a Senior Advisor - Mine Economics and Financial Modelling to join our advisory team in Newcastle or Brisbane, working at the intersection of investment analysis and mining operations. This is a hands-on analytical role focused on economic modelling, valuation and investment analysis of mining assets.



If you enjoy complex investment analysis and are wanting to broaden your skill base and build an important step for future career progression, this is the role that will do that.



About Us



Palaris is a specialist advisory firm to the mining and resources sector across the world. With over 5,000 projects delivered in over 50 countries across 30 different commodity types, we are the trusted advisor to resource companies, financial institutions, equity investors, and government agencies.



What You Will Gain



1. Work with real, high value assets

Work on mining projects involves billions in capital and decades-long horizons.

Get exposure to asset-heavy activities rarely seen in traditional PE/IB environments.

2. Stay transaction-focused - with more balance

Work on buy-side/sell-side due diligence, valuations, economic modelling and scenario planning.

Keep the analytical challenge of PE/IB, without the lifestyle trade-off (work a 9-day fortnight).

3. Develop technical insight that investors value

Learn how geology, mine planning, cost curves and processing constraints shape asset value - working directly with mining engineers, geologists and operational specialists.

Build expertise that opens doors to natural resources investing, corporate development or strategic roles inside major miners.

About the Role

Lead economic and financial modelling across full asset lifecycles

Build and interrogate economic models integrating geology, mine planning, processing and cost assumptions

Assess project economics, commodity sensitivities and capital structures

Integrate technical inputs into valuations and investment cases

Support investment and transaction processes from early screening through to negotiation and decision

Prepare clear, investment-ready reports and recommendations

Contribute to investment decision frameworks and technical due diligence for lenders, investors and mining companies

About You

Degree in Finance/Economics or Commerce

Solid experience from PE, IB or Transaction Services

Advanced financial modelling skills and excel expertise

Excellent reporting writing skills and presentation skills

Demonstrated analytical ability to build and interrogate economic models



No mining experience is required, we will immerse you in the world of mining, working alongside mining professionals to understand the technical drivers that shape asset value. Your investment perspective will add depth to our multidisciplinary team.



Interested?



If you are someone who often says “why not” – let’s have a conversation, call Chris Anderson, HR Manager on (02) 4911 2218 or apply now and we’ll be in touch.



Applicants must have full work rights for Australia, unfortunately, we are unable to offer visa sponsorship for this position.

As a Project Manager, you'll play a key role in the improvement of Government services through innovation, as we create the national property platform for Government. Working with Product & Engineering teams, you'll drive the successful implementation of VM Online - our Computer Assisted Mass Appraisal (CAMA) software, to Victorian Government from planning to delivery, and support an ongoing roadmap of upgrades and releases with the help of project and support teams.


12 month contract.

Put simply, our Data Solutions division is a true market leader! This is a unique opportunity to join a unique "business within a business", in a very supportive and close-knit culture with a huge pipeline of interesting and varied projects on the horizon to look forward to.


In a typical day you can expect to:


Lead end-to-end delivery of our SaaS implementation projects for government clients - owning scope, timelines, budget and quality while keeping everyone aligned and informed.

Partner closely with Product, Engineering and PMO teams to deliver high-impact outcomes using our hybrid Agile and Waterfall delivery practices.

Act as a trusted client-facing advisor - building strong stakeholder relationships and ensuring each project delivers real business value and ROI.

Drive governance and reporting frameworks that give clarity and confidence to our PMO and Executive teams.

Identify, manage and mitigate risks, issues and change requests - keeping projects moving smoothly and transparently.

Track project performance, financials and success metrics - documenting milestones and ensuring benefits are realised in line with the business case.

Plan and coordinate release activities - managing cross-team dependencies, resolving challenges and communicating release impacts clearly.

Contribute to a high-performing, collaborative team culture - embedding best practice, encouraging engagement and helping shape the future of government property technology.


Why Join Us:


We're the right-sized business for you to make a real impact, with a workplace culture where you can be you. There's so many perks! Here's some of the favourites…


Hybrid working between home, office and visiting clients.
Wellbeing Leave.
Leadership development programs, LinkedIn Learning and more.

Regular social events including our famous Innovation Days and annual BBQ!



Our Ideal Person:


ESSENTIAL:


A Bachelors degree or equivalent from an accredited university or college.

Proficient in Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC).

Experience delivering to Government or large corporate clients.

Experiencing working closely with Product and Technical teams.

Experience with Waterfall, Agile and Lean methodologies.

Superior communication and relationship management skills.

Highly productive, with the ability to lead and work both independently and as a team member.


NICE TO HAVES:


Agile Scrum or similar Project Management certification (preferred).

CoStar Group (NASDAQ: CSGP) is a leading global provider of commercial and residential real estate information, analytics, and online marketplaces. We have been living and breathing the world of real estate information and online marketplaces for over 35 years, giving us the perspective to create truly unique and valuable offerings to our customers. Job description:



The Opportunity: Be part of a team inspired by possibility.

Join Stockland's Customer & Digital team as a Senior Analytics Engineer, based at our Sydney Head Office. In this pivotal role, you will shape the future of our analytics capability by designing and delivering scalable data models and pipelines that power smarter decision making across the business.

You will lead a small analytics engineering team with 2 direct reports initially, with plans to grow the team over time, and partner closely with stakeholders across Platform Engineering, Business Intelligence, Software Engineering, Finance, Sales Operations and Commercial Property Analytics & Insights.

This is a permanent, full-time role with a hybrid working model. You will be in the office 3 days per week with anchor days on Tuesday and Thursday, and the third day flexible to what works for you and the team.

We're Looking For a...

You are an experienced Analytics Engineer or Data Engineer who enjoys working at the intersection of data, technology and business outcomes. You are comfortable leading others, setting direction for modern analytics engineering practices and working hands on with tools like dbt, SQL and AWS.

You thrive in collaborative environments, enjoy coaching others and are motivated by the opportunity to build and improve an evolving analytics function that has real impact across a large and complex organisation.

What You'll Do

Lead, coach and mentor a team of analytics engineers, creating an inclusive, supportive environment that encourages learning and high-quality delivery.
Scope, design and build robust, reusable data models and transformations using dbt within our AWS ecosystem to support BI, reporting, data science and analytical applications.
Develop and maintain enterprise grade data pipelines and analytical datasets, ensuring strong data quality, testing, documentation and governance.
Translate complex business requirements into scalable, domain driven data models, including ERDs and data flow diagrams aligned to enterprise standards.
Partner with teams such as Platform Engineering, Business Intelligence, Software Engineering, Finance, Sales Operations and Commercial Property Analytics & Insights to continuously improve analytics processes and deliver impactful data products.
Identify opportunities for automation, standardisation and optimisation across the analytics lifecycle, and help set the strategic direction and execution of analytics engineering at Stockland.

About You

You are passionate about turning data into trusted, usable products that enable better decisions. You enjoy working with stakeholders to understand context and needs, and you can explain technical concepts in a clear and accessible way.

You are looking for a role where you can lead a team, shape best practice, and stay close to the hands-on work, while contributing to an organisation that is focused on people, communities and sustainable outcomes.

Experience & Skills That Count

Demonstrated background in Analytics Engineering or Data Engineering, ideally within large or complex enterprise environments.
Strong hands-on experience with dbt and advanced SQL to design and deliver scalable, maintainable data models. dbt experience is essential.
Practical experience working with cloud-based data stacks, preferably AWS, with familiarity in Python, Git and CI/CD considered beneficial.
Proven capability in leading or mentoring technical team members, with strong collaboration, communication and stakeholder engagement abilities.
Comfortable interpreting and designing ERDs, data flows and database schemas, and contributing to improving data processes and standards.

The Right Mindset

A collaborative, inclusive leader who builds strong relationships and enjoys helping others grow.
Curious and improvement focused, always looking for better ways to design, build and deliver analytics solutions.
Comfortable working in an evolving, fast changing environment, with a strong sense of accountability for outcomes and value delivered to the business

The Stockland Proposition

We're a community: a supportive, empowered team that works as one to create better places and experiences for everyone, including ourselves. We're committed to building an inclusive workplace where diverse perspectives are welcomed, and everyone feels a genuine sense of belonging.

If you need adjustments during the recruitment process, just let us know.

Apply Today

At Australian Retirement Trust, we’re trusted by millions of Australians to deliver strong long‑term retirement outcomes. Our Investments team plays a critical role in achieving this, and we’re now seeking an Associate Portfolio Manager – Quantitative Enablement to strengthen our data, analytics and quantitative capability.

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. As an Associate Portfolio Manager - Quantitative Enablement, you’ll support the delivery of high‑quality data pipelines, analytics and technical infrastructure that underpin quantitative research, modelling and investment decision‑making across the Investment business. You’ll work closely with portfolio managers, quantitative researchers and data governance teams to ensure investment data is accurate, robust and fit for purpose.

You’ll join the Defensive and Liquid Assets and Portfolio Intelligence team, responsible for the strategic management of defensive and liquid assets, portfolio completion and opportunistic investments, as well as the data and analytics framework that supports ART’s investment insights and decisions

 

Day to day, you'll:

Perform logical and physical data modelling of investment processes.
Execute a structured data transformation process to deliver data products to investment teams.

Assist in the design and development of quantitative data pipelines and analytical tools to support investment decision‑making.
Implement systems and workflows to ensure the accuracy, completeness and consistency of investment data.
Maintain and enhance investment data quality, documentation and reproducibility.
Contribute to investment data strategy through in‑depth research, complex analysis and actionable insights.
Support standardised workflows to improve efficiency and reduce operational risk in data handling.
Collaborate with Quantitative Research & Analytics and Data Governance teams to align tools and infrastructure with strategic priorities.
Provide technical support to investment teams, including troubleshooting data issues.
Deliver performance updates and insights to stakeholders, contributing to clear and effective reporting and presentations.
Support governance, compliance and risk management obligations across the investment domain.

This role is a 7-month max-term/ secondment opportunity due to long service leave. 

📍 Location: Brisbane or Sydney.

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

You hold a Bachelor’s degree or Diploma in Business, Technology or a related field, providing a strong foundation for this role.
You bring hands‑on development experience designing, building and supporting data solutions using technologies such as Snowflake, DBT, Python or similar platforms.
You’re experienced working across analytics and software development life cycles and understand how robust processes support high‑quality outcomes.
You have solid experience in the investment industry or a relevant investment domain, with an understanding of market trends and emerging issues.
You demonstrate highly advanced research and analytical skills, enabling you to deliver deep insights that support investment decision‑making.
You’re a strong team player with well‑developed stakeholder management and negotiation skills, and you build trusted working relationships across the business.
You communicate clearly and confidently, and you’re comfortable presenting complex insights to senior stakeholders in both written and verbal formats.
You have experience working in CI/CD environments, using tools such as Azure DevOps and related systems.

Ad Text:
We are Coca-Cola Europacific Partners (CCEP) - 42,000 people across 31 countries, working together to make, move, and sell some of the world’s most loved beverages. As a leading global consumer goods company, we support 4 million customers and continue to invest in new products and innovation to delight the 600 million people who enjoy our drinks each day. 

In Australia, our portfolio includes the Coca-Cola family of products and well-loved brands such as Sprite, Fanta, Monster and Mother Energy, Mount Franklin, Powerade, Billson’s, and Grinders Coffee. 

About the Role… 

We have a fabulous opportunity to join a collaborative, friendly and successful team in this 12 month contract as Finance Business Partner (Coffee Sales).

Reporting directly to the Associate Director – Coffee Finance, the Finance Business Partner – Coffee Sales will be responsible for providing commercial sales support to the Head of Sales and National Field Sales Manager for our At Home/National and Operational channels along with their wider teams covering National Business Managers and State Managers. The role drives optimised EBIT outcomes and risk mitigation across both channels including our large contracted “Away From Home” customer base, along with contributing to Grinders Coffee long range plan.

 

What You’ll Be Doing...

Partnering with Sales team to deliver Grinders business performance targets through value-add analysis & insights on revenue to EBIT drivers including equipment and capital.

Responsible for preparing and managing the performance reporting processes across Grinders Sales - including month-end commentary, trading updates, reporting & insights.

Responsible for Sales and Operational Planning process in At Home and Away from Home in combination with Sales and Demand Planning teams and with assistance from Senior Analyst, providing risk-adjusted financial analysis and insights.

Responsible for working together with Shared Services Centre to deliver balance sheet management under Claims vs Accruals process providing risk and opportunity analysis across At Home customer accounts, including ensuring correct promo/terms accrual, rebate & pricing set ups.

Deliver commercial review and insights across Grinders customer agreement submissions in conjunction with Sales to maximise financial outcomes whilst identifying risks, recognising and solving contractual hurdles through analysis and modelling.

Influence key operational and strategic decisions through ad hoc analysis and projects.

Work effectively with business leaders to uplift commercial acumen.

Contribute to a high performing, collaborative and empowered team culture.

Collaborate across the Grinders team to help deliver division wide initiatives and projects and provide team coverage and support when required.




The ideal candidate will bring:



Extensive knowledge and understanding of commercial management with a minimum of 3-5 years of experience ideally in an FMCG environment.

Builds and maintains strong working relationships with key stakeholders understanding the objectives of all parties and identifying the associated risk and opportunities.

Strong influencing capability, ability to challenge in constructive manner leveraging data insights and drives mutually beneficial outcomes.

Strong communication and presentation skills with the ability to drive business outcomes and change by partnering with a diverse range of business stakeholders.

Advanced analytical thinking capability with proficient excel skills.

Cross functional experience supporting Sales & Marketing and Supply Chain is preferred.

Strong understanding of financial and commercial drivers as well as understanding of CCEP’s competitive environment.

University degree in Finance, Economics or Commerce related fields required.

SAP / Anaplan experience (prior system experience desired but not essential).

 

What’s in it for you?

Foot in the door opportunity to work with one of the world’s most recognisable brands.

Free monthly product allowances to share with family and friends (including Coca-Cola, Mount Franklin, Monster, and more!).

Access to our corporate benefits including Fitness Passport & discounts with our many diverse customers including Microsoft, Lenovo, Hertz, GoGet and Estee Lauder.

Free counselling for you and your family via our EAP helpline as part of our commitment to well-being.
